fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 77 is inverted. The clamping logic is executed when `clamp` is `False` and skipped when `clamp` is `True`, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: (y__4 != y__1) == (clamp and (y__1 < min(y0, y1) or y__1 > max(y0, y1)))

constraints.intent: The value should be changed if and only if clamping is enabled and the value is out of bounds.

spec.expr: y__1 >= min(y0, y1) and y__1 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: When clamp is true, the value should already be in bounds because this branch (incorrectly) does no clamping.
When clamp is false, the value should not be modified by the clamping logic in this branch.
